# Investment Memo — Series B in Northwind Data, Inc.

## Investment Thesis
We believe Northwind is on track to become the default observability layer for mid-market
data teams. The thesis is falsifiable: it wins only if net revenue retention stays above 115%
and gross margin holds above 70% as the company scales past $40M ARR. If retention slips below
that band the thesis breaks and we should not re-up.

## Company Overview
Northwind Data, Inc. is a Delaware C-corp selling a usage-based data-observability platform to
mid-market engineering organizations. The product has been generally available for three years
and is sold primarily through a product-led motion with an inside-sales overlay.

## Market
The data-observability market is expanding as data pipelines proliferate. Northwind targets the
mid-market segment underserved by enterprise-priced incumbents, where the buyer is a platform or
data-engineering lead rather than a central procurement function.

## Financials
Audited results support the core thesis. Annual recurring revenue of 42 million dollars was
reported for fiscal year 2025 [F1], up from the prior year. Gross margin of 71 percent was
reported for fiscal year 2025 [F2]. The company held 18 million dollars in cash and had no
outstanding debt [F3]. Net revenue retention was 118 percent for fiscal year 2024 [F5], and the
company employed 240 full time staff at the end of fiscal year 2025 [F6].

## Valuation
Our valuation uses a forward-ARR-multiple method benchmarked against a comparable set of
publicly traded and recently financed observability vendors. At the proposed round price the
implied ARR multiple sits inside the comparable range, and a discounted-cash-flow cross-check
under conservative retention assumptions supports the entry valuation rather than requiring
multiple expansion to clear our return bar.

## Risks
The principal risk is retention compression if a low-cost incumbent bundles observability into
an existing platform; the mitigant is Northwind's usage-based pricing and migration tooling that
raise switching costs. A second risk is key-person concentration in the founding team; the
mitigant is the founder ownership and vesting structure documented in the audited cap table [F4].

## Recommendation
We recommend investing in the Series B at the proposed price, subject to standard terms: a board
seat, a pro-rata right, and confirmatory diligence on the fiscal-year-2025 audited statements.

## Sources
- [F1] Audited income statement, FY2025 (audited-financial-statement; as-of 2025-12-31; US-Delaware).
- [F2] Audited statement of cash flows, FY2025 (audited-financial-statement; as-of 2025-12-31; US-Delaware).
